summary: Fair‑quality soundboard capture of an average‑to‑good 1979 show, highlighted by a scorching Passenger and solid set work.
review: The recording is a soundboard (SBD) with some mastering quirks – the pitch runs slow and needs about +3 % correction, and occasional audience bleed gives it a slightly “live” feel. Musically the night sits in the middle of the Fall‑79 tier: Jerry’s playing wavers at times but shines on Cold Rain, High Time and especially the blazing Passenger; Brent’s organ work is praised throughout, and Bob Weir steps up when Garcia struggles. Highlights include Passenger, Peggy‑O, Terrapin, High Time and Alabama, while Ship of Fools is noted as a weaker moment.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating from reviewers (≈3.4) combined with mostly fair soundboard quality results in a mid‑range AI score; the large number of consistent comments gives high confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Pitch runs ~3 % slow and needs correction; occasional audience bleed makes parts sound like an audience tape; overall decent clarity but mastering errors lower fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set leans cowboy‑style and a bit middling; the second set stretches out with ~40 min of post‑space jam, offering a mix of downtempo bluesy moments and high energy peaks (Passenger, Terrapin). Overall pacing is solid but not exceptional.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Inconsistent – occasional technical guitar problems and a laid‑back feel on some tunes, yet strong form on Cold Rain, High Time and the fiery Passenger.
band_member_comments.Bob: Steps up when Garcia struggles; praised for solid rhythm/vocal work during the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s organ/synth work is highlighted – “cutting loose” on Passenger, swirling organ on Cold Rain, synth‑filled Looks Like Rain, and overall strong contribution.
